articles.title: D.R. Horton (DHI) Faces New Questions As Forestar Cash Burn Raises Land Pipeline Risk
articles.summary: Forestar Group, the majority-owned land development subsidiary of D.R. Horton (NYSE:DHI), is facing higher risk tied to weak revenue growth and ongoing cash burn. Forestar's role in sourcing and developing lots for D.R. Horton links its financial health directly to the homebuilder's future land pipeline. Concerns around Forestar's cash usage raise questions about potential pressure on funding, land availability, and project timing for D.R. Horton. D.R. Horton is one of the largest...

articles.title: Can D.R. Horton (DHI) Still Be a Bargain After Raised Guidance?
articles.summary: D.R. Horton stock has returned 74.4% over the past five years, and the current checks suggest investors may still be paying less than its estimated intrinsic value, with both a Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) view and earnings multiples pointing to potential undervaluation despite a low overall value score. A 74.4% five year return signals that D.R. Horton has already rewarded patient shareholders, so any further upside case now leans more on valuation than on a turnaround story. Stronger...
